name: basic-rbac
description: Implement a basic RBAC policy in Rego with user-role and role-permission mappings

name: docker-authz-security
description: Write a Docker daemon authorization policy in Rego that prevents privileged containers and disabled seccomp profiles

name: http-api-authorization
description: Write an HTTP API authorization policy in Rego that decodes a JWT token and controls access based on method, path, and manager-subordinate relationships

name: terraform-iac-validation
description: Write a Terraform plan validation policy in Rego that prevents aws_security_group resources from allowing inbound SSH access from the internet

name: cloudformation-hook-policy
description: Write an OPA policy for an AWS CloudFormation hook that validates resource properties before deployment, using the CloudFormation hook input structure (input.resource.type, input.resource.properties, input.action with uppercase CREATE/UPDATE) and the package system response format

name: kubernetes-admission-control
description: Write a Kubernetes admission control policy in Rego using the kube-mgmt pattern (package kubernetes.admission, deny contains msg)

name: kubernetes-gatekeeper
description: Write a Gatekeeper ConstraintTemplate Rego policy using the violation rule with input.review.object and input.parameters

name: policy-metadata-annotations
description: Write a Rego policy with full OPA metadata annotations at package and rule level including title, description, authors, entrypoint markers, and custom fields

name: policy-testing
description: Write a Rego policy and tests using OPA test conventions including _test package suffix, input/data mocking with `with`, and deny message verification

name: http-api-rate-limiting
description: Write a Rego policy that enforces per-user API rate limits using `default rule := value` for tier-based fallbacks (premium, standard, default)

name: http-api-body-validation
description: Write a Rego policy that validates HTTP request body fields using set subtraction to detect unknown fields and explicit iteration for required field checks

name: regal-naming-conventions
description: Write Rego policies using correct naming conventions: snake_case identifiers, no get_/list_ prefixes on rule names, and no repetition of the package path in rule names

name: regal-iteration-style
description: Write Rego policies using `some x in collection` iteration style rather than `x := collection[_]` assignment-based iteration

name: regal-membership-operators
description: Write Rego policies using the `in` operator for membership checks rather than iterating and comparing with `==`

name: regal-function-style
description: Write Rego helper functions that take explicit arguments rather than referencing `input` or `data` directly (external-reference rule)

name: regal-defaults
description: Write Rego policies using `default rule := value` with the default declared at the top of the rule group, not using else branches or negation for fallback values

name: regal-boolean-structure
description: Write Rego policies using incremental set rules (`rule contains item if`) rather than comprehensions, and boolean rules without boolean literal assignments

name: regal-comprehensions
description: Write Rego policies using `object.keys()` for object key access and comprehension terms directly (not via intermediate variable assignment)

name: regal-bugs
description: Write Rego policies that avoid common bugs: use `x in collection` for membership (not `!=` in a loop), and match sprintf format string verbs to argument count

name: regal-testing-style
description: Write OPA tests in `_test.rego` files with `_test` package suffix, importing the policy package and referencing rules via the alias, with unique descriptive test names

name: regal-imports
description: Write Rego policies that import packages (not individual rules), avoid redundant aliases, and place all imports before any rule declarations

name: regal-annotations
description: Write Rego policies with metadata annotations at package and rule level, marking the entrypoint rule, with no blank lines between the metadata block and the rule
